chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# IVD Recipe: Discovery Before Intent
# ==============================================================================
# Version: 1.0
# Created: 2026-02-04
# Category: workflow_patterns
# Principle: 6 (AI as Understanding Partner) - extension "Discovery before intent"
# ==============================================================================

recipe:
  name: "Discovery Before Intent"
  version: "1.0"
  status: "experimental"  # Not yet validated in production; see canonization_process
  category: "workflow_patterns"
  created: "2026-02-04"
  author: "IVD Framework"

  description: |
    When the user doesn't know what to ask or lacks domain knowledge to give
    clear instructions, run discovery before the standard "describe → AI writes
    intent" flow. The AI proposes candidate goals, patterns (recipes), or
    options with short tradeoffs; the user selects or refines; then the same
    IVD flow applies. This is an optional step 0 under Principle 6 (AI as
    Understanding Partner), not a new principle.

  applies_to:
    - "User says 'I'm not sure what we need' or 'I don't know what to ask'"
    - "New domain or unfamiliar codebase"
    - "Exploring what's possible before committing to a direction"
    - "Onboarding: new team member needs to see options before describing"

  when_to_use:
    - "User cannot yet articulate a clear goal"
    - "Problem space is unfamiliar (new product area, new tech)"
    - "User wants to see 2–3 candidate directions before choosing"
    - "Existing codebase: user wants to avoid duplicating or to extend existing features"

  when_NOT_to_use:
    - "User already has a clear description of what they want (go straight to intent writing)"
    - "Trivial or well-defined task (e.g. fix typo, add unit test)"
    - "Fully specified requirement from stakeholder (no discovery needed)"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEPS (workflow pattern, not intent template)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

implementation_pattern:
  steps:
    - step: 1
      name: "Detect need for discovery"
      description: "User expresses uncertainty, or context is new domain / new codebase"
      signals:
        - "User: 'I'm not sure what we need'"
        - "User: 'What are the options?'"
        - "Vague hint only (e.g. 'something for compliance')"

    - step: 2
      name: "Gather options"
      description: "Use IVD tools to propose candidate directions"
      actions:
        - "ivd_list_recipes — list available patterns (classifier, workflow, infra, etc.)"
        - "ivd_list_features (if project_root set) — what already exists in repo"
        - "ivd_discover_goal (domain_or_context, user_hint) — get 2–3 candidate goals with tradeoffs"
        - "Optionally: ivd_propose_inversions(problem_description) for design alternatives (Principle 8)"

    - step: 3
      name: "Present and choose"
      description: "AI summarizes 2–3 options in one line each with short tradeoff; user picks or refines"
      output: "A chosen direction (e.g. 'Lead scoring clas

propose candidate directions"
      actions:
        - "ivd_list_recipes — list available patterns (classifier, workflow, infra, etc.)"
        - "ivd_list_features (if project_root set) — what already exists in repo"
        - "ivd_discover_goal (domain_or_context, user_hint) — get 2–3 candidate goals with tradeoffs"
        - "Optionally: ivd_propose_inversions(problem_description) for design alternatives (Principle 8)"

    - step: 3
      name: "Present and choose"
      description: "AI summarizes 2–3 options in one line each with short tradeoff; user picks or refines"
      output: "A chosen direction (e.g. 'Lead scoring classifier using agent-classifier recipe')"

    - step: 4
      name: "Proceed to intent flow"
      description: "Standard IVD: user describes (now with a direction) → AI writes intent → review → implement → verify"
      reference: "framework.md Principle 6 workflow"

best_practices:
  - "Keep discovery lightweight: 2–3 options, one-line tradeoff each"
  - "Don't write full intent in discovery; only choose direction"
  - "If user refines ('that one but for X not Y'), one more round is enough then go to intent"

common_pitfalls:
  - "Running discovery when user already knows what they want (wastes time)"
  - "Proposing too many options (overwhelms; keep to 2–3)"
  - "Skipping discovery when user is clearly stuck (then they guess, many turns)"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# REFERENCES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

references:
  principle: "Principle 6: AI as Understanding Partner"
  extension: "Discovery before intent (ivd_system_intent.yaml principle_6_ai_partner.extensions)"
  framework_section: "framework.md → When the User Cannot Yet Describe: Discovery Before Intent"
  implementation_guide: "framework.md → Step 0b (optional): Discovery When You're Not Sure What to Build"
  research: "research/discovery_before_intent.md"
